data.title: Calumet surges after securing $1.44B DoE financing for renewable fuels project
data.description:
Calumet (NASDAQ:CLMT) +22.3% post-market Wednesday after saying it was awarded a conditional commitment for a $1.44B loan guarantee from the U.S. Department of Energy Loan Programs Office to fund the construction and expansion of a renewable fuels facility in Montana.

The expansion would position the Montana Renewables unit as one of the world's largest sustainable aviation fuel producers, with production capacity of 300M gallons of SAF and 330M gallons of combined SAF and renewable diesel, Calumet (CLMT) said.

"This investment will allow us to leverage our first-mover advantage and unique renewable hydrogen and pretreatment technologies to transform Montana Renewables into a world scale SAF producer," Calumet (CLMT) CEO Todd Borgmann said.

data.title: Pfizer's Ibrance shows promise in treating appendix cancer: study
data.description:
• Pfizer’s (NYSE:PFE) breast cancer drug Ibrance, also known as palbociclib, showed encouraging results in the treatment of a certain type of appendix cancer, according to a new study.
  • The study showed that palbociclib was able to stabilize tumor growth and reduced blood tumor marker levels in patients with peritoneal mucinous carcinomatosis. The cancer, which is rare, is often resistant to standard chemotherapy.
  • The study was published in the Oct. 16 online edition of the Journal of Clinical Oncology.
  • Researchers said the study also showed Ibrance was able to slow or stop the multiplication of cancer cells. They added that the next step was to test the drug with chemotherapies and targeted drug therapies to see if its effectiveness could be enhanced, according to a statement.

data.title: Gevo skyrockets after securing $1.46B DoE financing for clean fuel project
data.description:
Gevo (NASDAQ:GEVO) +47.5% post-market Wednesday after saying it received a conditional commitment for a $1.46B loan guarantee from the U.S. Department of Energy Loan Programs Office for its Net-Zero 1 project in South Dakota.

NZ1 is designed to produce ~60M gal/year of sustainable aviation fuel, 1.3B lbs/year of protein and animal feed products, and 30M lbs/year of corn oil.

The design capability of the NZ1 facility is expected to yield sustainable aviation fuel with a net-zero carbon footprint on a lifecycle basis, including through the burning of the fuel, the company said.

NZ1 is the first-ever large-scale alcohol-to-jet project to receive a DoE LPO conditional commitment from the DoE LPO, and "marks a watershed moment for the Net-Zero 1 project and a critical step forward in Gevo's mission to transform the aviation industry by providing a scalable, sustainable, and economical renewable-carbon-based jet fuel - SAF," Gevo (GEVO) CEO Patrick Gruber said.

data.title: Oriental Rise Holding announces pricing of initial public offering
data.description:
• Oriental Rise Holding (ORIS) on Wednesday announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 1.75M ordinary shares at a public offering price of $4 per ordinary share, for total gross proceeds of $7 million.
  • The ordinary shares are expected to commence trading on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “ORIS” on October 17.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 18.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HCI Group expects to pay up to $750M to policyholders harmed by hurricanes
data.description:
HCI Group (NYSE:HCI) said Wednesday it expects to pay $600M-$750M to its policyholders following three major catastrophe events over the last few months: Hurricanes Debby, Helene, and Milton.

Net retained losses from Hurricanes Debby and Helene, after considering reinsurance recoveries and the reversal of benefits accrued under a multi-year reinsurance deal, are expected to result in a net expense to HCI (HCI) in Q3 of ~$60M.

Net retained losses from Hurricane Milton, meanwhile, is expected to result in a net expense to the company in Q4 of about $125M.

“The company is able to absorb these losses because of our strong balance sheet, our conservative reinsurance program with over $2 billion of occurrence reinsurance limit and over $3 billion of aggregate reinsurance limit, and our profitability,” said HCI Chairman and CEO Paresh Patel.

data.title: The Real Brokerage files to sell $100M of common shares
data.description:
• The Real Brokerage (NASDAQ:REAX) filed a prospectus for the offer and sale of $100M common shares offered by the company and 45M common shared offered by the selling shareholders.

data.title: Celularity acquires Rebound product from Sequence Lifescience
data.description:
• Celularity (NASDAQ:CELU) added to its commercial portfolio of placental-derived advanced biomaterial products with the acquisition of Rebound, a full thickness placental-derived allograft matrix product, from Sequence LifeScience.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Synovus Q3 earnings advance more than expected as interest income drives up
data.description:
Synovus Financial (NYSE:SNV) shares gained 3.1% in Wednesday after-hours trading after the lender posted Q3 earnings that rose more than expected as net interest income eked out a quarterly increase.

"As we move to a more favorable environment, I remain optimistic about our ability to accelerate growth given increased loan production and sustained strong core fee generation," said Chairman, CEO and president Kevin Blair.

Q3 adjusted EPS of $1.23, topping the $1.09 average analyst estimate, rose from $1.16 in Q2 and $0.84 in Q3 2023.

Adjusted preprovision net revenue drifted up to $262.3M from $261.7M in the prior quarter and $244.6M a year before.

Net interest income (taxable equivalent) was $442.1M, up from $436.3M in Q2 and down from $444.3M a year ago.

Net interest margin (taxable equivalent) of 3.22% vs. 3.20% in Q2 and 3.11% in Q3 of last year.

Provision for credit losses was $23.4M in Q3, compared with $26.4M in Q2 and $72.6M a year ago.
